div.p: get_mime_type(const char *path) — strrchr → linear scan → MIME string

g.text: static const mime_entry_t MIME_TABLE[] — 18 entries + sentinel

div.p: Performance: 19 entries × 16 B = 304 B total table size. Fits in 5 cache lines (320 B). Entire scan is L1-resident after first request. 19 × strcasecmp on short strings < 200 ns. strrchr on canonical path (≤ 4096 B): O(n) single pass, sequential.
Scans path right-to-left for the LAST occurrence of '.' Returns pointer into path string at position of the last dot. Returns NULL if no dot found.
For /var/www/html/assets/logo.png dot → ".png" (suffix substring) For .gitignore (hidden dotfile) dot → ".gitignore" (points to leading dot) → no table match → octet-stream

for i = 0; MIME_TABLE[i].extension != NULL; i++ if strcasecmp(dot, MIME_TABLE[i].extension) == 0 return MIME_TABLE[i].mime_type Case-insensitive: .PNG .png .Png all match. strrchr: .tar.gz → dot points to ".gz" (last dot).
/path/noext — strrchr returns NULL → early exit → application/octet-stream
/path/.gitignore — strrchr returns pointer to leading dot. ".gitignore" matches no entry → sentinel → application/octet-stream
/path/archive.tar.gz — strrchr finds LAST dot → dot=".gz". ".gz" not in table → application/octet-stream (not .tar)
/path/image.PNG — strcasecmp is case-insensitive. ".PNG" matches ".png" at index 9 → image/png
